lede: A look at August 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Chae Hyun-woo and Mike Conway, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Atlantic salmon
events.desc: Tens of thousands of farmed non-native Atlantic salmon are accidentally released into the wild in Washington waters in the 2017 Cypress Island Atlantic salmon pen break.

events.title: Canal Hotel bombing
events.desc: A truck-bomb attack on United Nations headquarters in Iraq kills the agency's top envoy Sérgio Vieira de Mello and 21 other employees.

events.title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
events.desc: Dissolution of the Soviet Union: The August Coup begins when Soviet President Mikhail Gorbachev is placed under house arrest while on holiday in the town of Foros, Ukraine.

events.title: Prime Minister of Japan
events.desc: Japanese prime minister Eisaku Satō becomes the first post-World War II sitting prime minister to visit Okinawa Prefecture.

events.title: North American B-25 Mitchell
events.desc: First flight of the B-25 Mitchell medium bomber.

events.title: 1934 German head of state referendum
events.desc: The German referendum of 1934 approves Adolf Hitler's appointment as head of state with the title of Führer.

events.title: Sioux Wars
events.desc: The First Sioux War begins when United States Army soldiers kill Lakota chief Conquering Bear and in return are massacred.

events.title: Charles Edward Stuart
events.desc: Prince Charles Edward Stuart raises his standard in Glenfinnan: The start of the Second Jacobite Rebellion, known as "the 45".

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Lobe den Herren, den mächtigen König der Ehren, BWV 137, a cantata setting the unchanged text of Neander's hymn.

events.title: Salem witch trials
events.desc: Salem witch trials: In Salem, province of Massachusetts Bay, Martha Carrier, George Jacobs Sr., John Proctor, John Willard, and clergyman George Burroughs are executed after being convicted of witchcraft.

births.name: Wright brothers
births.line: American engineer and pilot, co-founded the Wright Company (died 1948)

deaths.text: Abdul Rahim Hatif, Afghan politician, 8th President of Afghanistan
Linus Pauling, American chemist and biologist, Nobel Prize laureate
Jean-Baptiste Accolay, Belgian violinist, composer, and conductor
Alexander Jagiellon, Grand Duke of Lithuania (1492–1506) and King of Poland

observances.text: Afghan Independence Day, commemorates the Treaty of Rawalpindi in 1919, granting independence from Britain (Afghanistan)
Christian Feast Day: Ezequiél Moreno y Díaz
Christian Feast Day: Feast of the Transfiguration (Julian calendar), and its related observances: Buhe (Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church and Eritrean Orthodox Tewahedo Church)
